kpi_notes_im.kpi_01: 所選期間內記錄的事件總數。
kpi_notes_im.kpi_02: 所有事件中已解決（已完成狀態）的百分比。
kpi_notes_im.kpi_03: 仍處於未結或待處理狀態（尚未完成或取消）的事件的百分比。
kpi_notes_im.kpi_04: 目前處於未結或待處理狀態的事件數量。
kpi_notes_im.kpi_05: 已撤回或取消的事件數量。
kpi_notes_im.kpi_06: 標記有VIP代碼的事件的占比。
kpi_notes_im.kpi_08: 屬於重複房間＋類別＋項目組合的事件的占比。
kpi_notes_im.kpi_09: 事件建立到首次調查更新之間的平均分鐘數。
kpi_notes_im.kpi_10: 加權平均嚴重程度。低=1，中=2，高=3，嚴重=4。

kpi_notes_co.co_total_orders: 所選期間的清潔工單總數。
kpi_notes_co.co_completed_orders: 所選期間達到已完成狀態的清潔工單。
kpi_notes_co.co_completion_rate: 所選期間已完成清潔工單的占比。
kpi_notes_co.co_avg_duration: 已完成清潔工單的平均完成時長。
kpi_notes_co.co_median_duration: 已完成清潔工單的中位完成時長。
kpi_notes_co.co_on_time_rate: 已完成工單中按時完成的占比。
kpi_notes_co.co_delayed_orders: 已完成但超時的工單數量。
kpi_notes_co.co_reclean_rate: 被標記為返工的清潔工單占比。
kpi_notes_co.co_inspection_pass_rate: 通過質檢的已檢工單占比。
kpi_notes_co.co_productivity_score: 每工時產生的清潔積分。

hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_01: 所選範圍內的事件總案件數。良好：服務品質穩定且數值較低；不良：無需求增長背景下的持續增長超過前期的15%。
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_02: 人員配置與排隊規劃的營運工作負載基線。良好：可預測的數量且結案品質穩定；不良：超過計畫容量的波動峰值。
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_03: 在SLA規範規則內解決的事件的比例。良好 >= 95%；關注 90-94.9%；不良 < 90%。
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_04: 衡量活躍佇列中的乾淨結案品質。良好 >= 95%；關注 90-94.9%；不良 < 90%。
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_05: 事件後將服務回復正常狀態的有效性。良好 >= 95%；關注 90-94.9%；不良 < 90%。
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_06: 執行健康狀況的標準結案吞吐量KPI。良好 >= 95%；關注 90-94.9%；不良 < 90%。
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_07: 表明嚴重故障暴露的嚴重級別事件占比。良好 <= 1%；關注 1-2%；不良 > 2%。
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_08: 所有事件案件的平均嚴重度強度。良好 <= 1.8；關注 1.81-2.4；不良 > 2.4。
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_09: VIP影響事件的解決品質。良好 >= 95%；關注 90-94.9%；不良 < 90%。
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_10: 品牌保護監控的高端賓客事件暴露度。良好 <= 6%；關注 6-10%；不良 > 10%。
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_11: 跨儀表板版本趨勢連續性的相容舊版VIP風險占比。良好 <= 6%；關注 6-10%；不良 > 10%。
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_12: 集中於最高部門的程度；高集中度意味著瓶頸風險。良好 <= 30%；關注 30-45%；不良 > 45%。
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_13: 與未解決根本原因相關的復發熱點指標。良好 <= 15%；關注 15-25%；不良 > 25%。
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_14: 用於與歷史報告基線進行縱向比較的重複負載占比。良好 <= 15%；關注 15-25%；不良 > 25%。
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_15: 用於優先改善管道的最高投訴來源集中度。良好 <= 35%；關注 35-50%；不良 > 50%。
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_16: 目前未解決的未結工作負載壓力。良好 <= 5%；關注 5-10%；不良 > 10%。
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_17: 目前需要積極跟進處理的未解決佇列大小。良好：穩定在接近零的水準；不良：持續多天/多週增長。
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_18: 用於排班與人員配置的高峰時段負載標記。良好：每小時模式均衡；不良：與人員配置不一致的單一小時急劇峰值。
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_19: 從案件建立到首次調查更新的平均回應延遲。良好 <= 30分鐘；關注 31-60分鐘；不良 > 60分鐘。
hotel_im_kpi_notes.hkpi_20: 用於監控流程洩漏的已取消案件數量。良好 <= 2%；關注 2-5%；不良 > 事件總量的5%。

hotel_im_kpi_formulas.hkpi_03: (總案件數 - SLA違規案件數) / 總案件數 * 100
hotel_im_kpi_formulas.hkpi_04: 已完成案件數 / (已完成案件數 + 待處理案件數) * 100
hotel_im_kpi_formulas.hkpi_05: 已完成案件數 / 總案件數 * 100
hotel_im_kpi_formulas.hkpi_06: 已完成案件數 / 總案件數 * 100
hotel_im_kpi_formulas.hkpi_07: 嚴重案件數 / 總案件數 * 100
hotel_im_kpi_formulas.hkpi_08: 嚴重度評分總和 / 總案件數 (低=1, 中=2, 高=3, 嚴重=4)
hotel_im_kpi_formulas.hkpi_09: VIP已完成案件數 / VIP案件數 * 100
hotel_im_kpi_formulas.hkpi_12: 最高部門案件數 / 總案件數 * 100
hotel_im_kpi_formulas.hkpi_13: 重複事件案件數 / 總案件數 * 100
hotel_im_kpi_formulas.hkpi_14: 重複事件案件數 / 總案件數 * 100
hotel_im_kpi_formulas.hkpi_15: 最高投訴來源案件數 / 總案件數 * 100
hotel_im_kpi_formulas.hkpi_16: 待處理案件數 / 總案件數 * 100
hotel_im_kpi_formulas.hkpi_18: 高峰小時 = ARGMAX(小時事件數)
hotel_im_kpi_formulas.hkpi_19: AVG(首次調查時間戳 - 建立時間戳) 單位：分鐘

kpi_notes_jo.kpi_02: 成功完成的工單百分比。
kpi_notes_jo.kpi_03: 在SLA內完成的工單百分比。
kpi_notes_jo.kpi_04: 以逾時告終的工單百分比。
kpi_notes_jo.kpi_05: 升級處理請求干預的工單占比。
kpi_notes_jo.kpi_06: 跨團隊重新指派的工單占比。
kpi_notes_jo.kpi_07: 從建立到確認的平均分鐘數。
kpi_notes_jo.kpi_08: 回應時間的第90百分位數。
kpi_notes_jo.kpi_09: 從建立到完成的平均分鐘數。
kpi_notes_jo.kpi_10: 所有工單的總需求數量。

kpi_notes_mo.kpi_01: 所選範圍內的維修工單總數。
kpi_notes_mo.kpi_02: 成功完成的維修工單百分比。
kpi_notes_mo.kpi_03: 仍處於未結狀態的維修工單百分比。
kpi_notes_mo.kpi_04: 已取消的維修工單百分比。
kpi_notes_mo.kpi_05: 標記為賓客相關的維修工單占比。
kpi_notes_mo.kpi_06: 所有維修工單的平均嚴重度代理值。
kpi_notes_mo.kpi_07: 最大維修類別所占的份額。
kpi_notes_mo.kpi_08: 範圍內活躍的不同維修類別的數量。
kpi_notes_mo.kpi_09: 涉及的不同缺陷或資產組合的數量。
kpi_notes_mo.kpi_10: 所選期間內的日均維修工單量。

corp_kpi_notes.kpi_01: 綜合企業健康指數，平衡嚴重度、VIP暴露度與SLA違規風險。基準：良好 >= 85，關注 70-84.9，不良 < 70。
corp_kpi_notes.kpi_02: 分類為嚴重級別的事件的占比。基準：良好 <= 1%，關注 1-2%，不良 > 2%。
corp_kpi_notes.kpi_03: 用於公平連鎖級別比較的跨酒店平均基準指數。基準：良好 >= 85，關注 75-84.9，不良 < 75。
corp_kpi_notes.kpi_04: 涉及VIP賓客的事件的比例；追蹤高端服務風險。基準：良好 <= 6%，關注 6-10%，不良 > 10%。
corp_kpi_notes.kpi_05: 基於違規/延遲/逾期近似狀態的營運紀律KPI。基準：良好 <= 3%，關注 3-5%，不良 > 5%。
corp_kpi_notes.kpi_06: 已達到完成/結案狀態的事件的百分比。基準：良好 >= 95%，關注 90-94.9%，不良 < 90%。
corp_kpi_notes.kpi_07: VIP事件的解決效率。基準：良好 >= 95%，關注 90-94.9%，不良 < 90%。
corp_kpi_notes.kpi_08: 與忠誠度/留存風險相關的復發壓力指標。基準：良好 <= 15%，關注 15-25%，不良 > 25%。
corp_kpi_notes.kpi_09: 所選期間內的事件總數。基準：良好 <= 800，關注 801-1200，不良 > 1200（閾值應根據物業規模進行調整）。
corp_kpi_notes.kpi_10: 前5大類別中事件量的集中程度；較高則表示系統性集中風險。基準：良好 <= 45%，關注 45-60%，不良 > 60%。

corp_kpi_formulas.kpi_02: 嚴重案件數 / 總案件數 * 100
corp_kpi_formulas.kpi_03: 各酒店平均 [100 - (嚴重度*40 + VIP*30 + SLA*30)]
corp_kpi_formulas.kpi_05: SLA違規案件數 / 總案件數 * 100
corp_kpi_formulas.kpi_06: 已完成案件數 / 總案件數 * 100
corp_kpi_formulas.kpi_07: VIP已完成案件數 / VIP案件數 * 100
corp_kpi_formulas.kpi_08: 重複投訴案件數 / 總案件數 * 100
corp_kpi_formulas.kpi_10: 前5大事件類別案件數 / 總案件數 * 100

chart_notes_jo.hjo06: 按週升冪的工作負載（長條）與結案效率（折線）。影響：工單上升而結案率下降表明積壓風險。對策：增加短期產能，優先處理積壓工單，並強制執行每日結案目標直至比率穩定。
chart_notes_jo.cjo06: 按週升冪的工作負載（長條）與結案效率（折線）。影響：工單上升而結案率下降表明積壓風險。對策：增加短期產能，優先處理積壓工單，並強制執行每日結案目標直至比率穩定。
chart_notes_jo.hjo07: 按週升冪比較每週收件量與SLA表現。影響：高量週的SLA下降揭示了流程瓶頸。對策：部署應急方案，收緊交接SLA，每小時監控易違規佇列。
chart_notes_jo.cjo07: 按週升冪比較每週收件量與SLA表現。影響：高量週的SLA下降揭示了流程瓶頸。對策：部署應急方案，收緊交接SLA，每小時監控易違規佇列。
chart_notes_jo.hjo08: 每週逾時量趨勢，用於及早發現服務中斷。影響：逾時激增會降低賓客滿意度並增加重複聯繫。對策：確定根本原因週，修復路由/升級延遲，並設定逾時激增的警報閾值。
chart_notes_jo.cjo08: 每週逾時量趨勢，用於及早發現服務中斷。影響：逾時激增會降低賓客滿意度並增加重複聯繫。對策：確定根本原因週，修復路由/升級延遲，並設定逾時激增的警報閾值。

chart_bv_jo.jo-01: #1 · Demand Trends — identifies service categories growing in share week-over-week, enabling proactive staffing and preventive maintenance before peaks arrive.
chart_bv_jo.jo-02: #2 · SLA Health — tracks SLA compliance against weekly volume to expose bottlenecks during surge weeks before breaches compound.
chart_bv_jo.jo-03: #3 · Throughput Efficiency — closing rate versus weekly volume reveals operational backlog risk and drives daily closure target accountability.
chart_bv_jo.jo-04: #4 · Status Routing — drilldown from job status to service category exposes which work types are accumulating and driving queue congestion.
chart_bv_jo.jo-05: #5 · Demand Decomposition — drills into high-volume categories to surface the specific service items creating recurring strain.
chart_bv_jo.jo-06: #6 · Closure Efficiency — weekly workload versus closure rate trend exposes backlog build-up weeks requiring capacity intervention.
chart_bv_jo.jo-07: #7 · SLA vs Volume — compares incoming volume with SLA performance to detect process bottlenecks during high-demand periods.
chart_bv_jo.jo-08: #8 · Timeout Risk — weekly timeout trend enables early detection of service interruptions and routing delays.
chart_bv_jo.jo-09: #9 · Department Queue Health — status mix across top departments identifies which teams have the most congested queues.
chart_bv_jo.jo-10: #10 · Category Demand & Close Rate — spots high-volume low-close-rate categories for targeted performance intervention.
chart_bv_jo.jo-11: #11 · Item Concentration — ranks top service items by volume to identify recurring demand patterns requiring process standardization.
chart_bv_jo.jo-12: #12 · Department Load — shows assignment load concentration for rebalancing dispatch rules and cross-training.
chart_bv_jo.jo-13: #13 · Source Analysis — identifies departments creating the most JOs; signals upstream process gaps that can be fixed preventively.
chart_bv_jo.jo-15: #14 · Location Hotspots — reveals physical demand concentration to direct location-specific staffing and preventive actions.
chart_bv_jo.jo-16: #15 · Escalation Pressure — escalation by department shows where first-line resolution authority is breaking down.
chart_bv_jo.jo-17: #16 · Routing Inefficiency — reassignment by department quantifies triage quality and assignment accuracy gaps.
chart_bv_jo.jo-18: #17 · SLA Breach Magnitude — total breach minutes by category with item drilldown prioritizes fix effort where financial and reputational risk is highest.
chart_bv_jo.jo-19: #18 · Escalation by Category — identifies structurally escalation-prone service types for process redesign and training.
chart_bv_jo.jo-21: #19 · Resolution Duration Profile — department-level resolution time identifies long-tail execution delays and outlier teams.
chart_bv_jo.jo-22: #20 · Tail Completion — P90 resolution by category and item targets the worst-case completions that drive guest complaints.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-01: #1 · Cross-Hotel JO Volume — outer donut drilldown reveals which hotels generate the highest service demand by category across the chain.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-02: #2 · Status Distribution — hotel-by-hotel job status comparison exposes which properties have the worst queue congestion.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-03: #3 · Chain SLA Compliance — highlights SLA outlier hotels before complaints and penalties accumulate.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-04: #4 · Timeout Pressure — reveals hotels with disproportionate timeout rates signaling systemic routing or staffing problems.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-05: #5 · Escalation Rate — chain-wide escalation comparison identifies properties with the weakest first-line resolution capability.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-07: #6 · Service Mix — ranks top items chain-wide; click any bar to see its daily job count trend for demand pattern analysis.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-08: #7 · Response Speed — average response minutes across hotels benchmarks acknowledgment speed and front-desk efficiency.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-09: #8 · P90 Response — tail response time comparison identifies chronic slow-response hotels requiring process intervention.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-10: #9 · Resolution Duration — average end-to-end time by hotel surfaces completion efficiency gaps across the chain.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-11: #10 · Quantity Load — total quantity by hotel shows service demand concentration for chain resource allocation.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-12: #11 · Delay Analysis — reveals which hotels accumulate the most delayed jobs and which hours drive peak delay concentration.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-13: #12 · Completion Analysis — identifies which hotels complete the most jobs and which hours see peak completion activity.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-14: #13 · Timeout Analysis — pinpoints which hotels have the most timeout jobs and which hours contribute to service degradation.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-15: #14 · Status Mix — hotel-by-hotel status comparison shows which properties have the most imbalanced open/pending queues.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-16: #15 · Category Demand — top service categories by hotel identifies which properties have concentrated demand pressure.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-17: #16 · Item Demand — top service items across hotels drives chain-wide procurement and process standardization decisions.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-18: #17 · Department Origin Load — JO generation by department origin across hotels identifies systemic upstream gaps.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-19: #18 · Assigned Department Load — compares which departments are absorbing the most execution demand per hotel.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-21: #19 · Completion Throughput — which departments are completing the most JOs per hotel; drives accountability reporting.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-22: #20 · VIP Demand Clock — VIP job distribution by hour with service item drilldown reveals peak VIP service hours and the items most requested by VIP guests across the chain.
chart_bv_jo.jo-23: #21 · Hourly Completion Load — completed jobs by hour with duration drilldown surfaces peak completion times and longest-duration hours for shift staffing optimisation.
chart_bv_jo.jo-24: #22 · Hourly Response Load — acknowledged jobs by hour with response duration drilldown reveals when response latency is highest relative to demand.
chart_bv_jo.jo-25: #23 · Hourly Escalation Load — escalated jobs by hour with overdue duration drilldown identifies which hours produce the most severe SLA breaches.
chart_bv_jo.jo-26: #24 · Hourly Category Load — total jobs by hour with top item category drilldown reveals peak demand hours and which categories dominate each time slot.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-23: #21 · Completion Clock — completed job distribution by hour with duration-range drilldown reveals peak completion hours and whether fast or slow jobs dominate each time slot.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-24: #22 · Response Clock — acknowledged job distribution by hour with response-time drilldown identifies which hours have the slowest acknowledgment response across the chain.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-25: #23 · Escalation Clock — escalated job distribution by hour with overdue-duration drilldown surfaces the hours where SLA overruns are most severe chain-wide.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-26: #24 · Category Clock — total job distribution by hour with top item category drilldown reveals which hours and categories drive chain-wide demand peaks.
chart_bv_jo.jo-27: #25 · Status-Hour Load — job status volume with 24-hour drilldown reveals which statuses spike at specific hours, guiding shift allocation and escalation triggers.
chart_bv_jo.jo-28: #26 · Escalation-Hour Load — escalation group volume with 24-hour drilldown identifies when and which groups receive the most escalated jobs for targeted response planning.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-27: #25 · Chain Status Clock — job status distribution across the chain with 24-hour drilldown reveals which statuses and hours drive operational backlogs chain-wide.
chart_bv_jo.cjo-28: #26 · Chain Escalation Clock — escalation group distribution across the chain with 24-hour drilldown pinpoints which groups and hours concentrate chain-wide escalation pressure.

chart_bv_mo.mo-01: #1 · Property MO Volume Mix — outer donut shows total work orders by category; click to drill into job types, revealing where maintenance demand is concentrated for this property.
chart_bv_mo.mo-02: #2 · Status Distribution — work order status breakdown shows how much inventory is open, pending, or completed, helping managers clear backlogs before they affect guest experience.

chart_bv_mo.mo-09: #9 · Top Maintenance Categories — identifies which maintenance types occur most frequently to drive preventive maintenance planning.

chart_bv_co.co-01: #12 · Workload triage — reveals which room categories accumulate pending or in-progress orders; tells supervisors which room type to prioritise for early releases.
chart_bv_co.co-02: #5 · Labour model — compares check-out, stay-over, and vacant average cleaning times; every staffing and time-standard decision at property level rests on this differentiation.
chart_bv_co.co-03: #9 · Attendant speed — duration distribution per attendant with drilldown; primary coaching tool for identifying who needs mentoring vs who can mentor others.
chart_bv_co.co-05: #11 · SOP compliance — validates that full clean, turndown, and deep clean each meet their engineered time standards; deviations flag training or classification gaps.
chart_bv_co.co-06: #10 · Room-type SLA — confirms suites take longer than standard rooms; provides the evidence base for per-room-type time-standard negotiations with management.
chart_bv_co.co-07: #7 · Demand-quality — shows whether check-out rooms accumulate pending status disproportionately to pinpoint which demand type is causing the bottleneck.
chart_bv_co.co-08: #3 · Attendant ranking — identifies top performers for recognition and mentoring roles; surfaces low-volume staff for coaching or workload review.
chart_bv_co.co-09: #1 · Guest SLA — most direct measure of on-time room delivery; a large delayed bar means guests are waiting at check-in right now.
chart_bv_co.co-10: #2 · Quality cost — every re-clean consumes full labour with zero incremental revenue; inspection failures indicate training or equipment gaps.
chart_bv_co.co-11: #6 · Performance trend — daily volume and completion trend reveals whether the property is improving or degrading; feeds GM progress reporting.
chart_bv_co.co-12: #4 · Speed-punctuality — separates four archetypes: fast+on-time (benchmark), fast+delayed (scheduling), slow+on-time (over-staffed), slow+delayed (critical).
chart_bv_co.co-14: #8 · Floor productivity — shows which floors complete at which hours; reveals staffing imbalances and informs supervisor patrol and elevator-scheduling decisions.
chart_bv_co.co-19: #14 · Delay by hour — shows which hours produce the most delays; direct input for adjusting shift-start times and adding peak-hour reinforcement.
chart_bv_co.co-21: #13 · Stay-type distribution — full duration spread per stay type (not just average); P75 and P90 values set defensible SLA thresholds.
chart_bv_co.co-24: #20 · Duration spread by room — a wide spread for suites signals inconsistent SOPs; narrow spread confirms uniform room-type cleaning standards across attendants.
chart_bv_co.co-25: #15 · Delay-cause clock — shows which stay type generates delayed orders at which hours; enables targeted supervisor reinforcement at the right time.
chart_bv_co.co-28: #16 · Demand timing — shows when check-out vs stay-over cleans are completed through the day; drives early check-in feasibility and breakfast-shift sizing.
chart_bv_co.co-31: #17 · Delay hot-spot — identifies the specific hours producing the most delayed completions; primary input for shift-structure redesign.
chart_bv_co.co-34: #18 · SLA evidence — P50/P75/P90 duration by stay type; the document a property manager brings to a standards review to argue for differentiated SLA limits.
chart_bv_co.co-37: #19 · SLA calibration — shows the duration bracket where on-time orders flip to delayed; reveals whether the current SLA time limit is correctly set.
chart_bv_co.cco-01: #2 · Chain triage — shows which hotels accumulate incomplete orders in real time; first chart a corporate ops director opens before any other.
chart_bv_co.cco-02: #5 · Productivity benchmark — outlier hotels with slower average cleaning speed signal staffing gaps or process failures warranting GM performance review.
chart_bv_co.cco-04: #9 · Demand-mix — reveals each hotel's workload split by stay type; 80% check-out load requires a fundamentally different staffing model than 60% stay-overs.
chart_bv_co.cco-05: #12 · Asset mix — shows how room-type distribution drives per-hotel workload; feeds revenue-management SLA differentiation negotiations with property GMs.
chart_bv_co.cco-06: #7 · Output accounting — pairs credit with order count to surface high-volume/low-credit hotels indicating shortcuts or credit mis-classification.
chart_bv_co.cco-07: #3 · Executive ranking — most actionable C-suite chart; ranks hotels by verified cleaning output; dual-axis catches volume gaming through low-credit rooms.
chart_bv_co.cco-08: #1 · Chain SLA — most direct measure of on-time room delivery across all hotels; tall delayed bar means check-in failures are happening at that property today.
chart_bv_co.cco-09: #6 · Quality cost — chain-wide re-clean and inspection-failure data separates systemic (training/policy) failures from isolated property-level management issues.
chart_bv_co.cco-11: #8 · Speed-punctuality — four archetypes per hotel (crisis, over-staffed, scheduling issue, benchmark) guide intervention priority across the chain.
chart_bv_co.cco-15: #4 · Risk index — aggregates delay rate, re-clean rate, and credit gap into a single ranked composite per hotel; becomes the corporate intervention watch-list.
chart_bv_co.cco-16: #10 · Staffing peaks — shows which hotels hit cleaning demand peaks at which hours; informs cross-property float-staff deployment and AM shift boundary decisions.
chart_bv_co.cco-17: #11 · Quality leakage — quantifies rework burden per hotel; makes re-clean and inspection-failure costs visible and builds the business case for training investment.
chart_bv_co.cco-24: #14 · Stay-type calibration — actual duration spread by stay type across all hotels; the evidence base for chain-wide SLA time-standard differentiation.
chart_bv_co.cco-26: #15 · SOP validation — reveals whether full cleans, turndowns, and deep cleans meet time standards chain-wide; primary input to SOP review cycles.
chart_bv_co.cco-27: #13 · Room-type SLA — suite vs standard duration deviations chain-wide flag universal mis-classification or inadequate time standards across properties.
chart_bv_co.cco-31: #16 · Demand timing — shows when check-out demand peaks vs stay-overs across hotels; drives AM shift-boundary and early check-in policy decisions.
chart_bv_co.cco-32: #17 · Bottleneck timing — reveals at which hours pending orders pile up chain-wide; identifies systemic AM rush handoff failures across all properties.
chart_bv_co.cco-34: #18 · Delay hot-spot clock — shows which hours produce the most delayed completions chain-wide; sets escalation trigger windows and shift-design priorities.
chart_bv_co.cco-37: #19 · SLA target evidence — P50/P75/P90 cleaning duration by stay type across the chain; the document for corporate standards-committee meetings.
chart_bv_co.cco-40: #20 · SLA threshold check — shows the duration where on-time orders flip to delayed chain-wide; validates whether current SLA time limits are correctly set.

chart_formulas_mo.cmo01: COUNT(*) BY hotel_code, then TOP category BY hotel_code WHERE type = MO
chart_formulas_mo.cmo02: COUNT(*) BY hotel_code, then COUNT(*) BY job_status WITHIN hotel_code WHERE type = MO
chart_formulas_mo.cmo03: COUNT(*) BY created_date, hotel_code WHERE type = MO
chart_formulas_mo.cmo04: completed_orders / total_orders * 100 BY hotel_code WHERE type = MO
chart_formulas_mo.cmo05: open_orders / total_orders * 100 BY hotel_code WHERE type = MO
chart_formulas_mo.cmo06: Country Value = SUM(total_orders) GROUP BY country_code; Label = CONCAT(hotel_code, total_orders) list per country WHERE type = MO
chart_formulas_mo.cmo07: COUNT(*) guest_related vs non_guest_related BY hotel_code WHERE type = MO
chart_formulas_mo.cmo08: AVG(severity_weight) BY hotel_code WHERE type = MO
chart_formulas_mo.cmo09: COUNT(*) BY hotel_code, category WHERE type = MO
chart_formulas_mo.cmo10: MAX(category_count) / total_orders * 100 BY hotel_code WHERE type = MO
chart_formulas_mo.cmo11: COUNT(*) BY hotel_code, location WHERE type = MO
chart_formulas_mo.cmo12: COUNT(*) BY defect_or_asset WHERE type = MO

imc_chart_map.cim01: 酒店事件 → 前10事件項目
imc_chart_map.cim02: 事件總量 vs 狀態（依酒店）
imc_chart_map.cim03: VIP結案率 vs VIP事件（依酒店）
imc_chart_map.cim04: 酒店事件 → 前10事件類別
imc_chart_map.cim05: 連鎖 — 依酒店的重複事件率
imc_chart_map.cim06: 依酒店的事件世界地圖
imc_chart_map.cim13: 嚴重度 vs 數量象限圖

imc_chart_notes.cim01: 展示各酒店總數及前10事件項目，用於下鑽優先級排序。基準：良好 前3項目 <= 酒店事件的45%；不良 前3項目 > 60%（集中風險）。
imc_chart_notes.cim02: 比較酒店數量與狀態構成以檢測結案不均衡。基準：良好 已完成 >= 95% 且 待處理 <= 5%；不良 待處理 > 10%。
imc_chart_notes.cim03: 高端賓客風險與復原有效性的雙軸圖表。基準：良好 VIP結案率 >= 95%；不良 < 90%。
imc_chart_notes.cim04: 從酒店總數下鑽至前10類別，用於根本原因治理。基準：良好 最高類別 <= 20%；不良 最高類別 > 35%。
imc_chart_notes.cim05: 依酒店展示復發壓力，標記未解決的系統性問題。基準：良好 <= 15%；關注 15-25%；不良 > 25%。
imc_chart_notes.cim06: 附帶酒店標籤的國家級地圖，用於跨區域高階可見性。基準：良好 單一國家不超過連鎖事件的50%；不良 單一國家 > 70%。
imc_chart_notes.cim07: 酒店到部門下鑽，用於營運歸屬對齊。基準：良好 無部門超過酒店事件的25%；不良 > 40%。
imc_chart_notes.cim08: 酒店到來源下鑽，用於管道品質控制。基準：良好 未知來源 <= 5%；不良 > 15%。
imc_chart_notes.cim09: 依酒店堆疊比較VIP與非VIP負載。基準：良好 VIP占比 <= 6%；關注 6-10%；不良 > 10%。
imc_chart_notes.cim10: 從酒店總數下鑽至預訂來源構成，用於商業洞察。基準：良好 未知預訂 <= 5%；不良 > 15%。
imc_chart_notes.cim11: 在同一面板中比較風險、嚴重、VIP、SLA與趨勢的高階矩陣。基準：良好 風險評分 <= 60；關注 60-100；不良 > 100。
imc_chart_notes.cim12: 依加權風險對酒店進行排名，用於確定干預優先級。基準：良好 高風險酒店數量逐期減少；不良 最高酒店風險週環比增長 > 10%。
imc_chart_notes.cim13: 用於策略性風險分類的氣泡象限圖（高數量+高嚴重度=立即關注）。基準：良好 無酒店位於右上高風險象限；不良 多個酒店聚集在該象限。
imc_chart_notes.cim14: 區域矩陣比較嚴重、VIP、SLA違規與趨勢強度。基準：良好 所有風險儲存格趨勢下降或保持綠色；不良 同一區域有2個以上指標為紅色。
imc_chart_notes.cim15: 依酒店展示部門風險強度，用於針對性治理行動。基準：良好 最高部門風險 <= 酒店總量的20%；不良 > 35%。
imc_chart_notes.cim16: 對根本原因與累計貢獻進行排名，用於改善優先級排序。基準：良好 前5原因 <= 45%；不良 前5 > 60%。
imc_chart_notes.cim17: 依酒店突顯未解決的嚴重負擔（時效代理），用於升級治理。基準：良好 = 0個未結嚴重時效；不良 持續 > 3個案件。
imc_chart_notes.cim18: 跨酒店部門矩陣，用於快速基準對比與不均衡檢測。基準：良好 跨酒店差異平衡；不良 一個部門在多個酒店佔主導地位。

imc_chart_formulas.cim01: Level 1 = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY hotel_code; Level 2 = TOP 10 COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY incident_item_name per hotel
imc_chart_formulas.cim03: VIP Incidents = COUNT(vip_code valid) GROUP BY hotel; VIP Closure % = VIP Completed / VIP Incidents * 100
imc_chart_formulas.cim04: Level 1 = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY hotel_code; Level 2 = TOP 10 COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY incident_category per hotel
imc_chart_formulas.cim05: Repeat Rate % = repeat_count / total_cases * 100 per hotel
imc_chart_formulas.cim06: Country Value = SUM(total_cases) GROUP BY country_code; Label = CONCAT(hotel_code, incident_count) list per country
imc_chart_formulas.cim07: Level 1 = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY hotel_code; Level 2 = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY department per hotel
imc_chart_formulas.cim08: Level 1 = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY hotel_code; Level 2 = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY source_of_complaint per hotel
imc_chart_formulas.cim09: VIP = COUNT(vip_code valid); Non-VIP = total_cases - VIP; GROUP BY hotel_code
imc_chart_formulas.cim10: Level 1 = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY hotel_code; Level 2 = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY booking_source per hotel
imc_chart_formulas.cim13: X=COUNT(cases), Y=AVG(severity score), Bubble=VIP cases, Color=country/region
imc_chart_formulas.cim14: Regional KPI = AVG(metric by hotel in region); Regional Risk = aggregate of weighted KPI intensities
imc_chart_formulas.cim15: Department Risk Proxy = COUNT(cases) by hotel_code + department (or weighted severity where available)
imc_chart_formulas.cim17: Open Critical Aging Proxy = MIN(critical_cases, pending_cases) by hotel (aging date fallback when explicit age not present)
imc_chart_formulas.cim18: Matrix Cell = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY hotel_code, department
